revision.text:
[[ja:猪名部神社]] {{Expand Japanese|猪名部神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Inabe Shrine | native_name = 猪名部神社 | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Inabe Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.076115|136.577154}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Inabe Shrine'''|猪名部神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11378973|ltq=Q11378973|qid=Q11378973|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province}} ** Series ordinal: 214 ** P155: {{ill|Q135039434|ltq=Q135039434|qid=Q135039434|1=Tanaheno Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Totorinoyamatano Shrine|lt=Totorinoyamatano Shrine|qid=Q135039436|1=Totorinoyamatano Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q1204086|ltq=Q1204086|qid=Q1204086|1=Tōin, Mie}} * {{ill|Q864166|ltq=Q864166|qid=Q864166|1=Ise Province}} * {{ill|Inabe District|lt=Inabe district|ceb|Inabe-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی ئینابێ|en|Inabe District|es|Distrito de Inabe|fa|شهرستان اینابه، میه|fr|District d'Inabe|id|Distrik Inabe, Mie|it|Distretto di Inabe|ja|員弁郡|ko|이나베군|pl|Powiat Inabe|uk|Повіт Інабе|zh|員辨郡|qid=Q1131499|1=Inabe District}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.076115|136.577154}} == described by source (P1343) == * {{ill|Q135037878|ltq=Q135037878|qid=Q135037878|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)}} ** P2699: https://web.archive.org/web/20060722061240/http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp:80/db/jinja/070401.html == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181321 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Son-sha|lt=Son-sha|ja|村社|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Q11071125|ltq=Q11071125|qid=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q11123280|ltq=Q11123280|qid=Q11123280|1=Senior Fifth Rank}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q11071127|ltq=Q11071127|qid=Q11071127|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1190005008184 == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Winaheno Shrine|lt=Winaheno Shrine|qid=Q135039435|1=Winaheno Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '三重県いなべ市藤原町長尾', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:猪名部神社]] {{wikidata link|Q20043146}} == Japanese content == {{Image request|Shrine building, Ageuma Shinto ritual|date=July 2016|cat=Inbengun}} {{Shrine |Name = Inabe Shrine |Image = {{Looking for images|cat=Inbengun}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|qid=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Inabe-gun|Q1131499|qid=Q1131499|1=Inabe District}}{{ill|lt=Toin-cho|Q1204086|qid=Q1204086|1=Tōin, Mie}}Hokutaisha 796 |Position = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inabe clan|1=UNKNOWN}} ancestor, {{ill|lt=Ikairoo|Q55536799|qid=Q55536799|lt=Ikagairoo no Mikoto|1=Ikagashiko-o}},
{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Kenhaya Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|1=UNKNOWN}} |Company rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small), former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = |alias= |Regular Festival = Taisha Festival - Saturday and Sunday of the first week of April |Shinto ritual = Ageuma Shinto ritual |Map = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Inabe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Inabe Shrine'' (Inabe Jinja) is a shrine enshrined in {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|qid=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Inabe District|Q1131499|qid=Q1131499|1=Inabe District}}{{ill|lt=Toin Town|Q1204086|qid=Q1204086|1=Tōin, Mie}}{{Cite news|author=Rina Onishi|title=Many Successes Premonition of a good year Toin Inabe Shrine's Ageuma Shinto ritual|newspaper=Chunichi Shimbun morning edition|date=2016-04-03|edition=Mie General|page=27|language=Japanese}}. It is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and the old company rank is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. Member's speech {{ill|lt=Soja|Q1107129|qid=Q1107129|1=Sōja shrine}}. {{Cite web|title=Prefectural Shrine Office Inabe Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=63234|access-date=2025-12-26}} === History === The year of construction is unknown. {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōkan|1=Jōgan}} In the first year of the year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|qid=Q23897|1=859}}), he was granted the rank of {{ill|lt=Shikai|Q11591025|qid=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}Junior Fifth Rank, and in the same year ({{ill|lt=867|Q30347|qid=Q30347|1=867}}) he was given the rank of Junior Fourth Rank. On September 9th, 15th year of Jogan ({{ill|lt=873|Q30369|qid=Q30369|1=873}}), in the "Sandaime Jitsuroku", {{ill|lt=hand samurai|Q11498168|qid=Q11498168|1=Naishi-no-jō}}, who served the emperor, {{ja link|Harusumi Akiko|Q11513768|Harusumi Asaomi Akiko}} was given 1,500 bundles of government rice as travel expenses and dedicated it to Inabe Shrine. Since then, all shrine rituals have been held on the 9th day. {{ill|lt=Enki|Q1374325|qid=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}}5 years ({{ill|lt=905 years|Q24239|qid=Q24239|1=905}}), listed in {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}. === About Mr. Inabe === This Harusumi Takako (later renamed Akiko) was the eldest daughter of {{ill|lt=Council|Council|qid=Council|ltq=Q1133663}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daisuke Shikibu|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Harusumi Yoshina|Q11513769|qid=Q11513769|1=Yoshitada Haruzumi}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Seiwa|Q349243|qid=Q349243|lt=Seiwa|1=Emperor Seiwa}} , {{ill|lt=Emperor Yosei|Q349280|qid=Q349280|lt=Yosei|1=Emperor Yōzei}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Mitsukou|Q440518|qid=Q440518|lt=Mitsuko|1=Emperor Kōkō}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|qid=Q349267|lt=Uda|1=Emperor Uda}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Daigo|Q329753|qid=Q329753|lt=Daigo|1=Emperor Daigo}}. It is said that he especially had the trust of Emperor Uda.Zenna was {{ill|lt=Inabe clan|UNKNOWN|lt=Zou Inabe}}, and his grandfather {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inabezaimaro|1=UNKNOWN}} was {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|qid=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}{{ill|lt=Inabe County|Q1131499|qid=Q1131499|1=Inabe District}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=small territory|1=UNKNOWN}}, his father {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyoo Inabe|1=UNKNOWN}} was {{ill|lt=Suōkuni|Q1053037|qid=Q1053037|1=Suō Province}} large {{jalink|eyes}. (Kokushi)|Q11581532|He was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Junior Eighth Lower|1=UNKNOWN}}, the lowest rank among government officials. Zenkyu was intelligent and hardworking at a young age and rose through the ranks, and in {{ill|lt=Tencho|Q1373138|qid=Q1373138|1=Tenchō}} 5th year ({{ill|lt=828|Q30375|qid=Q30375|1=828}}), Zenkyu and his five brothers and sisters were given the surname of Harusumi Sukune. (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōkan}} In February 12 ({{ill|lt=870|Q23629|qid=Q23629|1=870}}), Harusumi Zenkyusaden, in addition to Takako, a female Zenkyoku, was given to the priests, as well as a man who had a strong attitude toward men (Junior Fifth Rank Upper {{ill|lt=Cuzenji) It is said that there were|Q11650312|qid=Q11650312|lt=Kuzen-cho|1=Kuzen-cho}} Kanesuo no kami, Gusei), {{ill|lt=Harusumi Uosui|Q79843706|qid=Q79843706|lt=Uosui|1=Uosui}} ({{ill|lt=Nuidonoryo|Q11608161|qid=Q11608161|lt=Nuidonokami|1=Nuidonokami}} Junior Fifth Rank Lower). Zennawa served during the three dynasties of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ninmyo|Q349271|qid=Q349271|lt=Ninmyo|1=Emperor Ninmyō}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Montoku|Q357225|qid=Q357225|lt=Buntoku|1=Emperor Montoku}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Seiwa|Q349243|qid=Q349243|lt=Along with Yoshio|1=Emperor Seiwa}} and {{ill|lt=Toyomichi Yasuno|Q17221777|qid=Q17221777|1=Yasuno no Toyomichi}}, he wrote {{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Koki|Q1041301|qid=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}, the fourth of the {{ill|lt=Rokukokushi|Q1151168|qid=Q1151168|1=Rikkokushi}}, and is referred to as ``Confucianism in the Dynasty'' in the "Fuso Ryakki". In the end, he died in the 12th year of Jogan ({{ill|lt=870|Q23629|qid=Q23629|1=870}}) while serving concurrently as the councilor of {{ill|lt=Junior Third Rank|Q11071123|qid=Q11071123|1=Junior Third Rank}}, Daisuke Shikibu, and the governor of Sanuki. Takako dedicated it to Inabe Shrine three years after her father's death.There are various theories about the origin of the Inabe clan. In the August 31st year of the Ojin era in the Nihon Shoki, the king of Silla presented his excellent craftsmanship to the Kingdom of Wa as atonement for the 500 imperial court ships that were being built and moored at Muko Sluice (Muko Minato) when a fire broke out in the Silla envoy's ship and the imperial court ships were completely burnt down. It is said that this family of craftsmen was the ancestors of Inabe. Tradition and duties of Inabe]. However, in the "Shinsei Shojiroku", it is written that the enshrined deity {{ill|lt=Ikairoo|Q55536799|qid=Q55536799|lt=Ikagashikio-no-Mikoto|1=Ikagashiko-o}} is the ancestral god of the Inabe clan, the older brother of the Tenson {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Keijoki-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, and the sixth-generation grandson of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Narahayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. In "{{ill|lt=Sendai Kūji Honki|Q2716751|qid=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}}" {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenjin Honki|1=UNKNOWN}}, it is said that five people offered offerings to Rokuhaya Hime when he descended from heaven. This is called “Gobujin” (Itsutomononokami), and they are * Amatsumara - the founder of {{ill|lt=Mononobe|Q55521285|qid=Q55521285|1=Mononobe officials}} * Amatsuyuso - Founder of {{ill|lt=Kasanibu|Q11599947|qid=Q11599947|lt=Kasanuibu|1=Kasanuimura}} etc. * Amatsu Akaura * Hohoro - {{ill|lt=Toichi-gun|Q11405036|qid=Q11405036|lt=Toichibe|1=Toichi district}}The ancestor of the heads* Amatsu Akaboshi - Tsukushi {{ill|lt=Genda District|UNKNOWN|lt=Genda}} Father of Mononobe et al. Of these, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Akaboshi|1=UNKNOWN}} or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Akaboshi|1=UNKNOWN}} is said to be the ancestor of the Inabe clan. According to the shrine's biography, the Inabe clan immigrated from around {{ill|lt=Inagawa|Q11571867|qid=Q11571867|1=Ina River}} in {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|qid=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province|1=Settsu Province}} (Hyogo Prefecture). {{ill|lt=Hokusetsu|Q17217833|qid=Q17217833|1=Hokusetsu}} region (currently {{ill|lt=Itami City|Q335444|qid=Q335444|1=Itami}}, {{ill|lt=Amagasaki City|Q213318|qid=Q213318|1=Amagasaki}}, {{ill|lt=Takarazuka City|Q823967|qid=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Ikeda City|Q817965|qid=Q817965|1=Ikeda, Osaka}}) was once the site of {{ill|lt=Ina Prefecture|Q11571881|qid=Q11571881|1=Ina no Agata}} during the {{ill|lt=Yamato Dynasty|Q2658252|qid=Q2658252|1=Yamato Kingship}} era, and Tamenatsuhiko{{ill|lt=Tamenatsuhiko Shrine|Q11567184|qid=Q11567184|1=Inatsuhiko Shrine}} History. It is said to have been ruled by a family headed by . However, the genealogy from here to Inbenzaimaro and Toyo is not clear. In {{ill|lt=Wado|Q1373026|qid=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}}6 ({{ill|lt=713|Q23523|qid=Q23523|1=713}}), by the imperial order of the 43rd {{ill|lt=Emperor Genmei|Q233224|qid=Q233224|1=Empress Genmei}}, the family name of Inabe was changed to 'Inabe'.Although there are no historical materials that directly link this Inabe to his origin, it is said that he had the skill of a craftsman in woodworking architecture, and in August 17th year ({{ill|lt=745|Q23612|qid=Q23612|1=745}}), the 45th emperor, {{ill|lt=Emperor Shomu|Q349263|qid=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}, established the Inabe family. For the construction of {{ill|lt=Todaiji|Q460367|qid=Q460367|1=Tōdai-ji}}, {{ill|lt=Momoyo Inabe|Q55536849|qid=Q55536849|1=Inabe no Momoyo}} from the Inabe clan was mobilized as a carpenter (=master carpenter), and {{ill|lt=Masuda Nawate|Q66364942|qid=Q66364942|1=Masuda no Nawate}}, a master craftsman from Hida, was mobilized as a carpenter (=deputy master carpenter). Furthermore, there are records that he was involved in the construction of {{ill|lt=Horyuji|Q261932|qid=Q261932|1=Hōryū-ji}}, {{ill|lt=Ishiyamadera|Q1082791|qid=Q1082791|1=Ishiyama-dera}}, and {{ill|lt=Kofukuji|Q1070863|qid=Q1070863|1=Kōfuku-ji}}, and from artifacts excavated from ancient tombs, it is known that he was also involved in the construction of {{ill|lt=Asukadera|Q779755|qid=Q779755|1=Asuka-dera}}. === Reitaisai === === Taisha (Oyashiro) Festival === The first weekend of April every year (excluding the first day of the month) - Although it is not classified as a major shrine, it has been called the Taisha Festival since its inception. === Ageuma Shinto ritual === Shiraku Festival, Honraku Festival, intangible folk cultural assets of Mie Prefecture from {{ill|lt=2002|Q1987|qid=Q1987|1=2002}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}}14). Run on a 100m horse track and run up a 2.5m high {{ill|lt=earthwork|Q1349587|qid=Q1349587|1=Earthworks (archaeology)}}. is a Shinto ritual to predict fortunes and fortunes. Riders in their late teens chosen from each of the four local regions spend a certain period of time {{ill|lt=purification|Q1936265|qid=Q1936265|1=Misogi}} in advance, and each race up the earthworks with their horses twice on the first day of the festival and once on the second day, and those who succeed sing kachidoki chants in the precincts. {{ill|lt=Takehisa|Q1193933|qid=Q1193933|1=Kenkyū}} December 3rd year ({{ill|lt=1192|Q19739|qid=Q19739|1=1192}}), {{ill|lt=Oki Castle|Q105260558|qid=Q105260558|1=Oki Castle}} Lord {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gunji|1=UNKNOWN}} It is said that {{ill|lt=Inabe Yukitsuna|UNKNOWN|lt=Inben Saburo Yukitsuna}} started it in Oinohara in order to improve the morale of young people after {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|qid=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} informed him of his desire for horse shooting and maki-gari. It dates back about 160 years to the Ageuma Shinto ritual at {{ill|lt=Tado Taisha|Q116140|qid=Q116140|1=Tado Taisha}} in the prefecture.It took its current form in {{ill|lt=Kenjin|Q1195433|qid=Q1195433|1=Kennin}}3 ({{ill|lt=1203|Q5227|qid=Q5227|1=1203}}), when it was falsely accused that Yukitsuna had assassinated the shugo of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|qid=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}. It is said that this was the work of the remnants of the Taira clan, {{ill|lt=Wakana Moritaka|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakana Goro Moritaka}}, who dedicates land in a corner of the precincts of Inabe Shrine for divine protection, built an ``age slope'' and dedicated raised horses and yabusame.Since {{ill|lt=2020|Q25337|qid=Q25337|1=2020}} (Reiwa 2), {{ill|lt=Social and economic impact of the 2019 coronavirus infection in Japan|Q86732769|qid=Q86732769|lt=New coronavirus The situation continues to be canceled due to measures to prevent the spread of infectious diseases|1=socio-economic impact of the COVID-19 pandemic in Japan}}, and in {{ill|lt=2023|Q49622|qid=Q49622|1=2023}} (Reiwa 5), only the ``Umahiki'' parade, in which people ride horses around the shrine grounds, will be held.{{Cite news |Japanese book |title="Ageba/Yasubime" held again this year at Toin's Inabe Shrine Taisha Festival |newspaper={{ill|lt=Chunichi Shimbun|Q904678|qid=Q904678|1=Chunichi Shimbun}} |date=2023-04-10 |url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/669340 |access-date=2023-12-07}}, Even after {{ill|lt=2024|Q49619|qid=Q49619|lt=next year|1=2024}}, only "Umahiki" will be held because "the number of people in the area who keep horses has decreased due to the coronavirus pandemic, and the know-how of Shinto rituals cannot be passed on."{{Cite news |Japanese book |title=Toin Town "Ageuma Shinto" ritual is not held; it is difficult to pass on the know-how due to the coronavirus |newspaper={{ill|lt=NHK Tsu Broadcasting Station|Q10854723|qid=Q10854723|lt=NHK Mie|1=NHK Tsu Broadcasting Station}} {{ill|lt=NHK News #Internet|Q10851844|qid=Q10851844|lt=NEWS WEB|1=NHK News}} |date=2023-12-04 |url=https://www3.nhk.or.jp/lnews/tsu/20231204/3070011826.html |access-date=2023-12-07}}. ==== Criticism ====As {{ill|lt=animal welfare|Q459426|qid=Q459426|lt=animal welfare awareness|1=Animal welfare}} has increased in recent years, there has been criticism that it is {{ill|lt=animal abuse|Q40053|qid=Q40053|lt=abuse|1=Cruelty to animals}}. |publisher={{ill|lt=Animal Rights Center|Q17191587|qid=Q17191587|1=Animal Rights Center Japan}} |date=2017-07-01 |accessdate=2023-05-12}} (Also refer to "{{ill|lt=Problems and countermeasures related to the Tado Festival #Ageuma Shinto ritual|Q116021|qid=Q116021|1=Tado Festival}}"), a prefectural investigation found that in {{ill|lt=2010|Q1995|qid=Q1995|1=2010}} (2010), one horse died on the spot after falling, {{ill|lt=2 In 011|Q1994|qid=Q1994|lt=the following year|1=2011}} and {{ill|lt=2015|Q2002|qid=Q2002|1=2015}} (2015), one animal was found to have been {{ill|lt=culled|Q652653|qid=Q652653|1=Animal euthanasia}} by a {{ill|lt=veterinarian#Japanese veterinarian|Q202883|qid=Q202883|lt=veterinarian|1=Veterinarian}} after suffering a broken bone.{{Cite news |Japanese book |title=Toin-cho Inabe Shrine's "age horse ritual" 3 horses die in 11 years |newspaper=NHK Mie NEWS WEB |date=2023-07-04 |url=https://www3.nhk.or.jp/lnews/tsu/20230704/3070010808.html |access-date=2023-11-03}}.Due to this, critical opinions have been received at Inabe Shrine and Toin Town Hall. === Precincts === Originally, there was a burial mound. {{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Toin Town History Volume 2 |publisher=Toin Town Board of Education |pages=377-387}} === Access === * {{ill|lt=Higashi-Meihan Expressway|Q5753692|qid=Q5753692|1=Higashi-Meihan Expressway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kuwana IC|1=UNKNOWN}} - {{ill|lt=National Route 421|Q6157502|qid=Q6157502|1=Japan National Route 421}} about 15 minutes (in Toin Town). * {{ill|lt=Sangi Railway|Q7418001|qid=Q7418001|1=Sangi Railway}}{{ill|lt=Sangi Railway Hokusei Line|Q7418003|qid=Q7418003|lt=Hokusei Line|1=Sangi Railway Hokusei Line}} {{ill|lt=Toin Station|Q7862665|qid=Q7862665|1=Tōin Station}} * {{ill|lt=Sangi Railway Sangi Line|Q17221021|qid=Q17221021|1=Sangi Railway Sangi Line}} {{ill|lt=Hokusei Chuo Koenguchi Station|Q11401181|qid=Q11401181|1=Hokusei Chūō Kōenguchi Station}} === Source === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === Reference materials === * {{Official|http://www.inabe-jinjya.or.jp/}} *
